updates.title: Redeploying Claude Fable 5: US Export Controls Lifted, Global Access Restored
updates.summary: The US Commerce Department lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 30, 2026, ending a 19-day suspension that began June 12 when a potential jailbreak technique was flagged in an Amazon security report. Anthropic restored global access to Fable 5 on July 1 across Claude Platform, Claude.ai, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork — with a new safety classifier that blocks the reported exploitation technique in over 99% of cases. For Pro, Max, Team, and select Enterprise subscribers, Fable 5 is included within 50% of weekly usage limits through July 7, after which it shifts to usage credits; access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry is being re-enabled.
updates.accuracy_notes: Highly verified — corroborated by Anthropic's official blog post, Anthropic's own X/Twitter announcement, CNBC (June 30), 9to5Mac, 9to5Google, VentureBeat, NBC News, Forbes, MarkTechPost, and Al Jazeera (all July 1, 2026). The June 12 suspension date, the Amazon jailbreak report trigger, the 99%+ classifier claim, and the July 7 credit changeover are all cross-confirmed across multiple independent outlets. No accuracy red flags.

instagram.hook: The AI the US government BANNED is back — and it just got safer
slides: Slide 1: What is Claude Fable 5? The most capable AI model Anthropic has ever released to the public — Mythos-class intelligence
Slide 2: Why was it banned? On June 12, a jailbreak technique was flagged. The US government issued an export-control directive — Anthropic took it offline for everyone
Slide 3: What Anthropic did in 19 days: Built a new AI safety classifier blocking the exploit technique in 99%+ of cases
Slide 4: July 1 — Fable 5 is BACK globally on Claude.ai, Claude Code, Claude Platform, and Claude Cowork
Slide 5: The catch: Free inclusion up to 50% of weekly limits until July 7 — then usage credits. Worth it? Absolutely.

updates.title: Anthropic Platform Release Notes: Billing Change, Model Retirements, SDK Updates (June 29 – July 1)
updates.summary: Anthropic published several platform and API updates in the 24-48 hour window: Claude Mythos Preview (claude-mythos-preview) was retired on June 30, 2026; fast mode was silently removed from Claude Opus 4.6 on June 29 (requests now run at standard speed); and fast mode for Claude Opus 4.7 has been deprecated with removal scheduled for July 24, 2026. On the billing side, requests that return stop_reason: 'refusal' without generating any output are no longer charged. Developers also gained REPL state persistence across code execution calls via SDK support for code_execution_20260120, and the MCP tunnels management API was moved from /v1/organizations/tunnels to /v1/tunnels with a new anthropic-beta: mcp-tunnels-2026-06-22 header.
updates.accuracy_notes: Good confidence — cross-confirmed from multiple secondary summaries of the Anthropic release notes (releasebot.io, web search returns referencing the official docs). Individual update dates (June 29 for Opus 4.6 fast mode, June 30 for Mythos Preview) are consistent across sources. The MCP tunnels API date (June 22) suggests that particular entry may be just outside the strict 48h window but was surfaced in the same release notes batch. No major accuracy concerns.
